title: coordinator/teammate: TeammateState transitions are not enforced — bare field access permits Dead->Running
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
solid
## Location
src/coordinator/teammate.rs:83-128 (TeammateState + Teammate struct)
## Violation
The module doc claims:

```rust
/// Lifecycle state. Transitions are one-way:
/// `Spawning → Running → Idle → Dead` and `Running → Dead` directly.
```

But the `Teammate` struct exposes the state as a `pub` field:

```rust
pub struct Teammate {
    pub id: TeammateId,
    pub agent_type: AgentType,
    pub color: AgentColor,
    pub state: TeammateState,
    ...
}
```

There is no `transition_to(...)` method, no invariant enforcement. The Phase 2 dispatcher (or any caller) can write `teammate.state = TeammateState::Running` after the teammate has already gone `Dead`, violating the documented contract silently. The same risk applies to setting `state = TeammateState::Idle` from `Spawning` without ever passing through `Running`.

This is LSP-adjacent: any Phase 3 wrapper assuming the contract will be wrong.

## Mandated refactor
Make the `state` field `pub(crate)` (or private) and add:

```rust
impl Teammate {
    pub fn transition_to(&mut self, next: TeammateState) -> Result<(), InvalidTransition> {
        if !self.state.can_transition_to(&next) { return Err(...) }
        self.state = next; Ok(())
    }
}
```

with a corresponding `TeammateState::can_transition_to` that enforces the documented transition table.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
